name: Deaf or Hard of Hearing User
description: Sound carries information that may not be visible on the page. Videos play without captions, audio-only instructions assume hearing, and sound-based alerts have no visual backup. For deaf and hard of hearing users, equal access means every audio moment is also text. Captions aren't optional—they're navigation.

checks.message: Videos require captions or a transcript. Without them, deaf and hard of hearing users miss dialogue, context, and meaning. Captions should be synchronized with audio and include speaker identification and sound descriptions.
checks.technical: Add <track kind='captions'> inside <video>, or embed captions directly. Captions should include dialogue, speaker names, and descriptions of significant sounds (e.g., [door slams], [music plays]).

checks.message: Audio-only content (podcasts, recordings, sound effects) must have a transcript or visual alternative. Deaf users cannot access audio without text.
checks.technical: Provide a transcript alongside all <audio> elements. For audio embeds, link to a text transcript. Describe sound events that convey meaning.

checks.message: Captions are present. Ensure they are accurate, synchronized, and include speaker identification and sound descriptions.
A transcript is available. Ensure it is complete, includes speaker names, and describes significant sounds or music.
If the page uses sound to convey information (alerts, notifications, status changes), provide a visual or text alternative. No hearing user should be required to hear the page.
checks.technical: Never rely on sound alone. Pair audio cues with visual indicators, text messages, or aria-live announcements.

checks.message: Alerts that rely on sound (beeps, chimes, sirens) must also have a visual indicator (color change, animation, icon) and accessible text. Hard of hearing users need to see alerts, not just hear them.
checks.technical: Use aria-live regions for alerts. Pair audible alerts with visual feedback and text announcements.

checks.message: Notifications delivered only through sound are inaccessible to deaf users. Use visual + text alternatives.
checks.technical: Display notifications visually (toast, banner) with text content. Do not rely on sound alone.

checks.message: Podcasts and audio content require transcripts. Deaf listeners should be able to read every word.
checks.technical: Provide a full written transcript alongside every podcast episode. Format clearly and link obviously.
